tools.name: list_dimensions
tools.title: List guidance dimensions
tools.description: List the 14 guidance dimensions used by the Security Career Assessment matching engine (id, bilingual name and description, content status).

tools.name: list_assessment_questions
tools.title: List assessment questions
tools.description: List the 16 Security Career Assessment questions. Each question includes id, type (single | multi | rating), bilingual prompt and topic, options (for single/multi) and scale bounds (for rating). Answer IDs from this list are what `compute_career_matches` accepts as input.

tools.name: list_professions
tools.title: List professions
tools.description: List the pilot security-industry professions supported by the Career Center and matching engine. Returns id, slug, bilingual titles, family, content status, whether the role is regulated, and the profile status. Use `get_profession` for full detail.

tools.name: get_profession
tools.title: Get profession
tools.description: Return the full Career Center record plus the matching-engine target profile for one profession, looked up by id or slug (e.g. 'security-manager', 'ordningsvakt'). Includes bilingual titles, description, responsibilities, competencies, target dimensions, gate, and regulatory notes.

id.description: The profession id or slug, e.g. 'security-officer' or 'security-manager'.

tools.name: compute_career_matches
tools.title: Compute career matches
tools.description: Run the Security Career Assessment matching engine against a set of answers and return ranked career matches. Input is a map from question id (q1..q16) to an answer value: option id string, array of option ids, 1..5 rating, or null. Use `list_assessment_questions` to discover valid ids. Results include displayed match indicator (0..100), confidence (limited | moderate | stronger), top contributing dimensions, gaps, and the user dimension vector. The indicator is guidance only, not a probability of professional success or eligibility.

answers.description: Object keyed by question id (e.g. 'q1') with answer values.

topN.description: How many top matches to return (default 5). Values below 1 or above the profile count are clamped.
